channel.title: Frontion News
channel.description: Strategic analysis of the events reshaping the world.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Long War Sets In: Blockades, Collapsing Currencies, and the Fracturing of Order
item.description: As the Iran war enters its third month with no end in sight, the geopolitical shockwaves are spreading far beyond the Strait of Hormuz — from OPEC's fragmentation to central banks scrambling on inflation, from Congress grilling the Pentagon to Gaza's expanding occupation zones.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Blockade War: Hormuz, Pipelines, and the Fracturing Global Order
item.description: As Iran and the US trade ship seizures in the Strait of Hormuz and Europe unblocks €90 billion for Ukraine, the war's economic ripple effects are reshaping global logistics, energy markets, and diplomatic alignments from the Gulf to the Taiwan Strait.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Deal Takes Shape: Trump Claims Final-Stage Negotiations with Iran as Hormuz Control Solidifies
item.description: President Trump announced that US-Iran negotiations have entered the 'final stage' and a deal could be reached within days. But on the ground, Iran has consolidated de facto control of the Strait of Hormuz — imposing checkpoints, diplomatic clearances, and in some cases fees on vessels transiting the world's most critical oil chokepoint.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: G7 Backs Iran Deal as Hormuz Reopening Nears, Fed Holds, and Lebanon Burns
item.description: The G7 closes with unanimous support for Trump's Iran memorandum — but the deal's terms remain contested, Israel keeps striking Lebanon, oil markets brace for a slow recovery, and the Fed signals inflation worries under its new chair.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Beijing Summit: Trump and Xi Face Off Amid Iran's Ceasefire on Life Support
item.description: As Trump arrives in China with tech CEOs in tow, the Iran ceasefire frays and global energy markets brace for impact — while Putin's forces pound Ukraine and UK politics face meltdown.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: US-Iran Escalation Tests Ceasefire as Xi Courts North Korea
item.description: Tit-for-tat strikes between US and Iran threaten fragile peace efforts after Apache helicopter downing, while Xi Jinping's Pyongyang visit signals China's effort to reassert influence on the Korean Peninsula amid deepening Russia-North Korea ties.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Ceasefire's Crucible: Hormuz Reopening Tests Fragile Peace as Global Trade Wars Resume
item.description: The US-Iran ceasefire faces its most critical test as American forces attempt to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while Trump's renewed tariff threats against Europe add fresh fuel to an already volatile global landscape.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: Iran Strikes Gulf States as Ukraine Hits St Petersburg on Putin's Forum Opening Day
item.description: The Iran-US war entered a dangerous new phase with IRGC missile strikes on Kuwait and Bahrain, while Ukraine launched a major drone attack on St Petersburg as Putin's flagship economic forum opened. The parallel escalations highlight the interconnected instability facing the US and its allies.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Cartel Cracks: UAE Abandons OPEC as Gulf Crisis Reshapes Global Energy
item.description: A seismic realignment of Middle Eastern power is underway as the UAE exits OPEC, Iran's Revolutionary Guards consolidate wartime authority, and the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked—all while Mali's collapse opens a new front of instability in Africa.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Clock Runs Down on Hormuz
item.description: As the U.S.-Iran ceasefire deadline approaches, all eyes turn to Islamabad where last-ditch negotiations could determine whether the Strait of Hormuz reopens or the world economy takes a hit. Meanwhile, East Asia reads the writing on the wall.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Hour Before War: Trump Pauses Iran Strike as Gulf Allies Intervene
item.description: President Trump revealed he was one hour from ordering fresh strikes on Iran before Gulf states persuaded him to pause. Tehran has submitted a new peace proposal, but the gap between US demands and Iranian terms remains wide.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: US-Iran Deal Signed, NATO Adjusts to US Force Cuts, China Asserts Claims in East China Sea
item.description: The US-Iran peace agreement formally takes effect as Tehran reopens the Strait of Hormuz, while NATO scrambles to fill gaps left by planned US force reductions and Beijing escalates maritime pressure on Taiwan and Japan.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The China Summit: Trump's Beijing Gamble as Iran Ceasefire Hangs by a Thread
item.description: As Trump heads to Beijing for high-stakes talks with Xi, the Iran ceasefire teeters on collapse and US inflation surges to 3.8% — a convergence of diplomatic, military, and economic pressures that will define the coming week.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Fraying Ceasefire: Israel Strikes Lebanon as Trump Claims Final-Stage Iran Deal
item.description: Israel's strike on Tyre kills eight despite Iran's warning, a US Apache crashes near Hormuz, and Ukraine's drone campaign reaches Moscow. Trump says an Iran deal is days away, but the ground war keeps escalating.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Ceasefire That Isn't: Hormuz on the Brink as Trump's Project Freedom Meets Iranian Resistance
item.description: A fragile US-Iran ceasefire frays under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, while the UAE comes under missile attack for a second day and the Red Sea geopolitics realign with a US-Eritrea rapprochement.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: US-Iran Deal in Doubt as Rubio Rules Out Sanctions Relief for Hormuz Reopening
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told Congress the US will not trade sanctions relief for access to the Strait of Hormuz, signaling a harder line as Iran's Fars News reports diplomatic communications have halted. Meanwhile, Russia struck Kyiv with a massive drone and missile barrage after a week of threats, and Ukraine's battlefield momentum continues to build.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Blockade Stalemate: Oil Soars, Ceasefires Fray, and Europe Rearms
item.description: As Iran's Hormuz blockade enters its ninth week, oil prices breach $126 a barrel and diplomatic paths narrow. Meanwhile, Europe accelerates its defense revolution and Ukraine navigates a new Russian ceasefire proposal—while the world braces for the economic fallout of a prolonged energy crisis.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Strait Standoff: Trump's 'Shoot to Kill' Order Escalates Gulf Crisis
item.description: As Trump orders the US Navy to fire on Iranian boats laying mines and Tehran seizes vessels in retaliation, the war of blockades intensifies—while inside Iran, a fractured leadership navigates economic collapse and internal power struggles.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: Diplomacy's False Floor: Pakistan Mediates US-Iran Talks While Asia Pays the Price
item.description: Six weeks into the US-Iran ceasefire, Pakistan is scrambling to revive stalled peace talks while the economic damage spreads across Asia and the Middle East. Iran is reviewing Washington's latest proposal, but neither side is bending — and the countries bearing the costs have no seat at the table.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Deal's First Day: US-Iran MOU Takes Effect as Ukraine Strikes Moscow and Hegseth Shakes NATO
item.description: A 14-point memorandum of understanding between the US and Iran entered force on Thursday, starting a 60-day negotiation clock — but Israel's expanded Lebanon occupation, Ukraine's largest drone attack on Moscow, and a Pentagon review of US forces in Europe all underscored how much remains unresolved.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Multipolar World Isn't Coming — It's Here
item.description: Iran reshapes American power, alliance reliability, and economic leverage. China studies the playbook for Taiwan. Europe prepares for defense without US guarantees. Russia exploits the distraction.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: Beijing Summit: Trump and Xi Find Common Ground on Hormuz as New Front Opens
item.description: The Trump-Xi summit produced agreement on keeping the Strait of Hormuz open and a major Boeing deal, but Xi's blunt warning on Taiwan and a new ship seizure by Iran underscored the limits of diplomacy. Meanwhile, Israel-Lebanon ceasefire talks resumed in Washington, and the US Senate blocked another attempt to constrain Trump's Iran war powers.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: Trump Threatens Seizure of Iran's Oil Hub as Ceasefire Unravels
item.description: US-Iran ceasefire strains to breaking point after second day of strikes, with Trump threatening to 'take' Kharg Island and control Iran's oil industry. G7 leaders brace for Iran-dominated summit as ECB hikes rates to combat energy-driven inflation.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: Hezbollah Rejects Ceasefire as US House Challenges Trump's Iran War Authority
item.description: A US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire collapsed within hours after Hezbollah rejected it, while the US House voted to limit presidential war powers — complicating Trump's Iran negotiations. Oil prices eased on deal hopes, but the path to ending the Hormuz crisis remains blocked.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: Ceasefire Extension Tempers Hormuz Crisis as Taiwan Frets Over US Arms Delay
item.description: A 60-day ceasefire extension between the US and Iran has eased immediate tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, but key questions remain unanswered — including whether Iran will retain de facto control of the waterway. Meanwhile, delays in a $14 billion US arms sale to Taiwan and Tomahawk shipments to Japan are raising doubts about US reliability in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: Alliance Fractures: NATO in Crisis as Trump Targets Spain, Europe Charts Independent Path
item.description: The Iran war's diplomatic shockwaves are tearing at NATO's foundations—Spain faces suspension threats, Britain and France are urged to lead a European defense coalition, and global energy markets face historic disruption as the Hormuz standoff continues.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: Switzerland Talks Open Amid Hormuz Closure, Lebanon Ceasefire Shatters, Starmer Set to Resign
item.description: US-Iran negotiations resume in Switzerland but Iran's second closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Israeli strikes killing at least 20 in Lebanon after a ceasefire underscore how fragile the peace remains. Meanwhile, Keir Starmer appears set to resign as UK Prime Minister, and Ukraine warns of a massive incoming Russian attack.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The Last Chance Diplomacy
item.description: As the US-Iran ceasefire enters its final days, negotiations shift to Pakistan while markets brace for what comes next. From Sofia to the Strait of Hormuz, the ripple effects are already being felt.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: Three Fronts Intensify: Ukraine Strikes Moscow, Ebola Spreads, and Iran's Demands Grow
item.description: Ukraine launched its largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year as Pakistan shuttles new Iranian demands to Washington. A WHO-declared Ebola emergency in Congo and Uganda adds a global health crisis to an already strained international system.

item.title: Evening Strategic Brief: The War of Endings: Ceasefires Test Two Fronts as Peace Takes Shape
item.description: Iran responds to US ceasefire proposals while Putin declares Ukraine 'coming to an end' — but both conflicts face spoilers, violations, and critical unresolved issues that could unravel diplomatic progress.
